novel_features.name: Named config profile capture
novel_features.description: Capture your whole router config as a named, reusable profile.
novel_features.rationale: Only possible by serializing the UCI tree + GL module configs into a local store with a model/firmware provenance stamp; the GL UI has no named/versioned profiles.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants to bank a known-good config before changing anything on the road.

novel_features.name: Version+model-gated restore
novel_features.description: Restore a saved profile, with a safety check that it matches this device.
novel_features.rationale: Replays stored UCI and checks the snapshot's model/firmware provenance — warns on firmware drift, refuses on model mismatch without --force, mirroring sysupgrade's same-model lock.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to revert cleanly to a standard config without risking a mismatched-device restore.

novel_features.name: Option-level config diff
novel_features.description: See exactly which settings a venue changed vs your standard config.
novel_features.rationale: Parses UCI change-tuples into the local store and diffs at option granularity, snapshot-vs-current or snapshot-vs-snapshot — no single API call provides this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to answer 'what did this hotel network change?' before reverting.

novel_features.name: WiFi region diagnose + fix
novel_features.description: Find out why a foreign network is invisible and fix the regulatory domain in one command.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-references an iwinfo scan against a static country->allowed-channels table to name the country that would unhide a network, then sets the regdomain and reloads.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when repeater mode can't see a network abroad (e.g. EU 2.4GHz ch12/13 under a US regdomain).

novel_features.name: Venue onboarding macro
novel_features.description: One command to get online at a new venue: scan, region-check, join, and prep for captive portals.
novel_features.rationale: Orchestrates repeater scan -> region check -> join -> captive-portal prep (MAC clone, drop VPN/AdGuard DNS) -> restore as a single sequenced macro over existing endpoints.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use on arrival at a hotel/cafe to skip the multi-step LuCI dance.

novel_features.name: VPN toggle + egress verify
novel_features.description: Start a VPN tunnel, arm the kill-switch, and confirm your public IP actually changed.
novel_features.rationale: Combines tunnel start + kill-switch + a before/after public egress-IP check; returns a typed exit code on leak.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to trust that the VPN is really protecting traffic, not just 'connected'.

novel_features.name: Connection fidelity / leak check
novel_features.description: Verify the VPN is up and your connection isn't leaking: egress country, DNS leaks, and STUN/UDP (WebRTC-style) leaks.
novel_features.rationale: Combines real egress geolocation, a STUN/UDP public-IP probe, a DNS-leak test, and the router's VPN client status into one verdict with a typed exit code on leak; no single endpoint provides this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to confirm a VPN is actually protecting all traffic (no IP/DNS/WebRTC leak) before trusting it on an untrusted network.

novel_features.name: Connectivity troubleshooter
novel_features.description: Diagnose why the router has no internet and get the exact fix command.
novel_features.rationale: Walks a decision tree across netmode, WAN/internet, repeater source, cable, tethering, DNS, kill-switch, and region; names the most likely cause and the command to fix it (--fix auto-applies safe remedies).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use first whenever the router is up but there's no internet — most often it's not connected in repeater mode.

novel_features.name: Source-uplink quality + improvements
novel_features.description: Diagnose a weak/slow venue WiFi uplink and get ranked ways to improve it.
novel_features.rationale: Joins iwinfo info/scan/assoclist + repeater status into RSSI, band, link rate/PHY, channel congestion and latency, then ranks improvement suggestions; local metrics only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you have internet but it's bad and you want to know whether to move, switch bands, or tether.

novel_features.name: Doctor / capability probe
novel_features.description: Report this router's model, firmware, OpenWrt/LuCI versions, reachable surfaces, and per-feature availability.
novel_features.rationale: Probes get_info, openwrt_release, opkg, and /rpc vs SSH reachability to show exactly what this firmware/model supports before you act.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to confirm what a given firmware supports before relying on a feature.

novel_features.name: Current-config summary
novel_features.description: A structured, per-subsystem report of the router's current configuration.
novel_features.rationale: Renders live uci show + GL status modules into a wifi/wan/vpn/firewall/lan digest in one read.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to quickly understand or capture the current state of a router.

novel_features.name: Config option search
novel_features.description: Find any config option anywhere across the whole router config tree.
novel_features.rationale: FTS over captured snapshot sections/options locates an option across the UCI tree + 43 GL modules in one query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to locate where a setting lives when config is spread across dozens of modules.

novel_features.name: Raw passthrough escape hatch
novel_features.description: Call any GL RPC module/function or any UCI option directly.
novel_features.rationale: Thin authed transport over the hand-built GL /rpc and SSH uci layers reaches any of 43 modules or any UCI option the CLI doesn't wrap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as the power-user fallback for anything not covered by a typed command.

novel_features.name: WAN source switch
novel_features.description: Switch the router's WAN source and verify it reconnected.
novel_features.rationale: Sets the netmode/WAN source then confirms reconnect + internet state in one step.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when changing how the router gets upstream internet at a new venue.

narrative.headline: The first maintained CLI for GL.iNet travel routers — named config profiles, travel macros, and version-aware diagnostics no other GL.iNet tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: Save a known-good 'home' config, adjust it at a hotel or abroad, then revert in one command with snapshot save/apply/diff. Get online at a new venue and fix the 'my foreign network is invisible' regulatory-domain problem instantly. Every command probes your exact model and firmware first, so it adapts instead of assuming — and it all runs locally over the router's own API, no GoodCloud.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authenticates against the router's local admin password via GL's challenge/crypt/sha256 handshake (read from the live challenge, so it works across firmware versions). Config snapshots and UCI access use SSH to the router. No cloud account needed.

quickstart.comment: confirm reachability, model, firmware, and what this device supports
bank your current known-good config as a named profile
see the current configuration at a glance
diagnose 'router up but no internet' and get the fix
after a venue changes things, see exactly what differs from home
revert cleanly back to your standard config

troubleshoots.symptom: Login fails / 'access denied' from the router
troubleshoots.fix: The admin password is the router's web-UI password (RPC user is 'root'). Set GL_INET_PASSWORD or pass --password.

troubleshoots.symptom: Repeater can't see a network abroad
troubleshoots.fix: Run 'gl-inet-pp-cli wifi region diagnose' — the network is likely on a channel your current regulatory domain forbids; it will name the country to switch to.

troubleshoots.symptom: snapshot apply refuses with a model mismatch
troubleshoots.fix: Snapshots are model+firmware stamped; restore on the same model, or pass --force to override (settings may not map cleanly).
Those use SSH (port 22). Ensure SSH is enabled on the router LAN and your key or password works.

narrative.when_to_use: Use gl-inet for managing a GL.iNet travel router from the terminal or an agent: capturing and reverting config profiles while traveling, getting online at new venues, fixing regulatory-domain repeater problems, diagnosing 'no internet' and weak-uplink situations, and version/model-aware inspection of what a given firmware supports. It is local-only (the router's own API + SSH), with no GoodCloud dependency.

recipes.title: Bank home, travel, revert
recipes.command: gl-inet-pp-cli snapshot save home && gl-inet-pp-cli snapshot diff home && gl-inet-pp-cli snapshot apply home
recipes.explanation: Capture a known-good profile, see what a venue changed, then revert cleanly.

recipes.title: Get online abroad
recipes.explanation: Scan, region-check, join, and prep for the captive portal in one command.

recipes.title: Fix an invisible foreign network
recipes.explanation: Detects a network hidden by your regulatory domain and switches the region to unhide it.

recipes.title: Why is there no internet?
recipes.explanation: Walks the connectivity decision tree and applies safe remedies (most often: not joined in repeater mode).

recipes.title: Narrow a verbose status payload for an agent
recipes.explanation: Use --select with dotted paths to return only the fields an agent needs from a large status response.
